title: hordeum sylvaticum var. europaeum
intro: Hordeum sylvaticum var. europaeum, also known as wild spelt, is a type of wild grass that belongs to the same genus as domesticated spelt, and is characterized by its distinctive growth habit and seed heads. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. Consuming wild spelt has been associated with several health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. Wild spelt can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used as a dietary supplement.
